finding: Regulation overtakes EA average as Slovak cost pressures ease marginally
bullets: Regulation rose marginally to 6.18/10 (n=225), up 0.12 points from wave 37, exceeding the EA average of 5.89 — the only problem among the seven tested where Slovakia scores above the euro area, as EA scores eased by 0.15 points, suggesting Slovak firms now face relatively greater regulatory burden compared to peers.

Costs of production or labour scored 6.19/10 (n=228) — the highest among the seven problems tested for Slovak firms — easing marginally by 0.15 points from wave 37, yet remaining below the EA average of 6.48, indicating some relief but still a dominant concern.

Access to finance was the only problem to worsen for Slovak firms, rising 0.19 points to 3.91/10 (n=223); at below 5/10, it remains a low priority in absolute terms, though it diverges from the EA and DE trends, which both eased, signalling potential tightening conditions for Slovak firms.
